name: Ruisleipa (Sour Rye Bread)
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Warm water or potato water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the sour bread starter and 2 cups warm or potato water. Mix in 1 cup of rye flour. Cover lightly and let stand in a warm place for about 20 to 40 hours, depending on desired sourness.
2. Dissolve yeast in 1/4 cup warm water, then stir into the soured mixture. Gradually add the remaining rye flour, mixing well after each addition.
3. Add the sifted white flour gradually, beating thoroughly after each addition until a stiff dough forms. Let rest for 10 to 15 minutes.
4. Turn onto a floured board and knead until smooth. Place the dough in a slightly greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover and let rise until doubled in bulk.
5. Divide into two parts. For Eastern-Finnish style loaves, shape each piece into a ball and place on a lightly greased baking sheet. Let rise until almost doubled, about 30 minutes.
6. For Western-Finland style flat loaves, shape the dough into two balls and flatten by patting to about 1-inch thick, keeping the shape round. Make a hole in the center about 2 inches in diameter. Dust lightly with rye flour. Place rolls on a lightly greased baking sheet. Prick all over the top with a fork. Let rise until doubled in bulk, about 30 minutes.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 45 minutes or until lightly browned.
Sour bread starter:
About 2 days before making sour rye bread, mix 1/2 cup milk with 1/2 cup rye flour. The milk must be at room temperature. Let stand uncovered in a warm place until the mixture begins to bubble and has a pleasantly sour odor. Refrigerate if needed or use immediately in the bread dough.
